alignments.pub_claim_text: The prism-based approach enables estimation regardless of the initial and true parameter values, even in cases where the original RL model cannot be estimated due to the numerical problem. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Prism-RL model achieved better fit and prediction performance than the RL model, by implicitly restricting paths with large detour or many loops. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Prism-RL model achieved better fit and prediction performance than the RL model, by implicitly restricting paths with large detour or many loops. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over identically into the publication with the same emphasis on fit and prediction gains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Prism-RL model demonstrates the possibility of describing more realistic route choice behavior. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fourier neural operators (FNOs) perform significantly better than comparable deep learning approaches for learning solution operators of partial differential equations (PDEs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fourier neural operators (FNOs) perform significantly better than comparable deep learning approaches for learning solution operators of partial differential equations (PDEs). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FNOs can achieve speed-ups of multiple orders of magnitude over conventional numerical PDE solvers once trained. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FNOs can achieve speed-ups of multiple orders of magnitude over conventional numerical PDE solvers once trained. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over this speed-up claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model-parallel version of FNOs can predict time-varying PDE solutions of over 2.6 billion variables using up to 512 A100 GPUs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model-parallel version of FNOs can predict time-varying PDE solutions of over 2.6 billion variables using up to 512 A100 GPUs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model-parallel FNO was successfully trained on the Azure cloud for simulating multiphase CO$_2$ dynamics in the Earth's subsurface. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model-parallel FNO was successfully trained on the Azure cloud for simulating multiphase CO2 dynamics in the Earth’s subsurface. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical training and application claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Multi-level Graph Relational Reasoning Network (MGRR-Net) enhances the discrimination ability of facial action unit (AU) features by performing multi-level feature learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Multi-level Graph Relational Reasoning Network (MGRR-Net) improves facial action unit detection by incorporating multi-level feature learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fused features from region-level, pixel-wise, and channel-wise learning lead to improved AU discriminative ability. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MGRR-Net approach achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on the DISFA and BP4D AU datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that MGRR-Net achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on the DISFA and BP4D AU datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most studies focused on developed countries, with none conducted in African countries. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of studies focused on high-income countries, with no studies conducted in African countries. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a rapid development of XR technology for mental health care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a rapid development of XR technology for mental health care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: GNNs encounter significant performance degradation when the amount of supervision signals is limited. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-supervised learning methods can outperform GNNs trained with few labeled nodes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-supervised methods fall short of learning class discriminative node representations due to the lack of labeled information during training. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed semi-supervised method GraFN achieves better node classification than both semi-supervised and self-supervised methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic spatiotemporal decompose GC (DSTD-GC) uses only 28.6% of the parameters compared to the state-of-the-art graph convolution methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic spatiotemporal decompose GC (DSTD-GC) uses only 28.6% of the parameters compared to the state-of-the-art graph convolution methods.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DSTD-GC improves representation capability by relaxing certain constraints of other graph convolution methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing differential privacy practices in deep learning suffer from limitations in objective perturbation, gradient perturbation, and output perturbation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing differential privacy practices in deep learning suffer from limitations in objective perturbation, gradient perturbation, and output perturbation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework demonstrates improved performance in terms of accuracy loss and privacy leakage against black-box membership inference attacks.
